title: Resolver / codegen / generated driver
description: The downstream consumers ADR 0004 reserves for post-freeze: the schema-aware resolver, the type-safe code generator, and the generated driver wiring. Each is written once against the stable frozen model. Per ADR 0004 §Decision.

title: Freeze query.Query + revise ADR 0003 + CONTEXT.md
description: When Stages 1-5 are complete, lock the query model. Revise ADR 0003 to declare the model frozen and document its final shape. Update CONTEXT.md glossary to the final terminology. Also specify the resolver's API shape in the freeze ADR: schema-bound constructor (`resolver.New(schema).Resolve(query)`) per the deferral agreed during Stage 2 grilling — see ADR 0006 §Consequences ("Resolver API shape is deferred"). ValidatedQuery lives in the resolver package. This is the gate ADR 0004 names: no consumer is built until this lands.

title: Stage 1 — non-property-typed parameters (SKIP/LIMIT $p)
description: First model evolution after Stage 0: parameters whose type comes from their slot rather than a binding property. Specifically bare $p in SKIP and LIMIT. Introduces query.Use as a sealed sum and relaxes invariant D4.
design:
Unlock bare SKIP $p / LIMIT $p as non-property-typed parameter uses.

Model change:
- Introduce sealed sum `query.Use` = PropertyUse | PositionalUse (mirrors Binding/Endpoint discipline)
- PropertyUse wraps a Ref (existing case)
- PositionalUse carries a `Position` enum (PositionSkip | PositionLimit), int-backed, stringer (mirrors graph.EntityKind)
- `Parameter.Uses` type changes from []Ref to []Use
- Invariant D4 relaxes: every Use is exactly one of PropertyUse | PositionalUse
- Smart constructors enforce invariants
- Tagged-union MarshalJSON with lowercase "kind": PropertyUse flattens to {"kind":"property","variable":...,"property":...}; PositionalUse to {"kind":"positional","position":"skip"|"limit"}

Accept/reject delta from Stage 0:
- SKIP $p (bare): reject → accept as PositionalUse{Skip}
- LIMIT $p (bare): reject → accept as PositionalUse{Limit}
- SKIP $p + 1, LIMIT f($p), ORDER BY $p, every other deferred-parameter construct: stay rejected
- Accept rule mirrors WHERE D1 directness discipline: bare positional only

Dedup unchanged: Parameter dedup by Name in first-appearance order. Mixed-kind uses on one Parameter are fine; the resolver judges type unification post-freeze (parser stays schema-agnostic per ADR 0003).

JSON casing wart: Parameter struct stays uppercase Go-default while Uses items go lowercase tagged-union. Acknowledged scope-creep-avoidance — defer harmonization to post-freeze.
acceptance_criteria:
- TCK scenarios in clauses/return-skip-limit/ exercising SKIP $p / LIMIT $p flip red→green
- Layer 2 targeted sentinel tests cover still-rejected cases (SKIP $p+1, ORDER BY $p)
- No previously-passing scenario regresses; pending count only decreases
- Both gated-swarm reviewers (Linus + Sandi) approve the diff
- `just test` green
notes:
Plan agreed via /grill-with-docs on 2026-05-26.

Reference docs:
- ADR 0004 — test-first, evolving-until-feature-complete (governs staged approach)
- ADR 0003 — schema-agnostic parser; resolver handles type unification
- ADR 0005 — generated code executes original text; model is type-interface only
- docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-0.md — Stage 0 spec (canonical baseline)

Deliverables in the same PR as the code:
- docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-1.md — short delta-style spec doc (~80-120 lines); structure mirrors Stage 0
- CONTEXT.md gains a "Use" entry; "Parameter" refined to reference it
- Stage 0 spec §4 C2 erratum: one-line note clarifying that label-conflict against the schema is the resolver's responsibility (the conjunctive constraint interpretation is faithful to openCypher, not a parser limitation)

TCK dir widening: add clauses/return-skip-limit/ to the godog dir list. Expect 1-2 skiplist additions to surface during implementation (runtime-error assertions we cannot model).

Implementation methodology: /tdd inside /gated-swarm (Carmack implements; Linus + Sandi review).
close_reason: Stage 1 feature-complete: model evolution (Use sum + ClauseSlotUse), parser change (mineClauseSlotParameter for SKIP+LIMIT bare $p), TCK corpus widening (return-skip-limit/ + 18-entry skiplist + TestSkiplistOrphans), Layer-2 rule update (directional rule + AUTHORED pin for non-bare $p in SKIP/LIMIT), documentation bundle (Stage 1 spec + CONTEXT.md Use entry + Stage 0 §4 C2 note). All five cycles approved by both gated-swarm reviewers (Linus + Metz). just test green at 509/315/0/194.

title: Cypher query.Parser: feature-complete the read surface
description: Umbrella for the staged build-out of internal/query/cypher. Per ADR 0004: evolve query.Query through Stages 1-5 (test-first against the openCypher TCK), then lock the model and build the resolver / codegen / generated driver. No consumer is built until after the freeze.

title: Resolver: undirected edge two-orientation trial + multi-match semantics
description: Post-freeze resolver contract handed off by Stage 5 (gqlc-7z6). An EdgeBinding with directed=false has NO authoritative orientation: the parser records endpoints in textual order only. The resolver MUST form TWO candidate schema.EdgeKeys (source->target AND target->source) and accept the binding if EITHER resolves against the directed-only schema. OPEN QUESTION (spec cypher-query-parser-stage-5.md §9): the trial can match zero, one, or TWO distinct directed edge types (schema has both A->B and B->A with the queried label) — define whether a double match is ambiguous, a union, or an error, and whether the resolver must report WHICH orientation(s) matched (which may require widening the marker bool->enum, cheap pre-consumer). Lives downstream of resolver bead gqlc-0mx and the freeze gqlc-cta.

title: Stage 3 — projections & aggregations
description: Extend RETURN beyond bare-variable / single-property: expressions, aggregates (count/sum/collect/...), literals, function calls, RETURN *. ReturnItem becomes an expression sum. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
notes: Stage 3 on branch stage-3-projections (single branch, separated commits). DONE: (1) grill-with-docs -> spec docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-3.md committed (d652c11). (2) MODEL commit f053af8 PUSHED: ReturnItem.Value Projection sealed sum + AggregateFunc enum + Query.ReturnsAll + JSON + 77 goldens; Linus gate PASS; pure refactor (180 pending unchanged), just test green. NEXT sub-step = UNLOCK commit: listener classifies projections (Ref/Literal/Func/Aggregate) + accepts RETURN * (set Query.ReturnsAll), add skiplist group per spec §6 (NoVariablesInScope/UnknownFunction/ColumnNameConflict), Layer-2 swaps (+2 mustParse, -1 return-star mustReject, +1 authored residual ErrUnsupportedProjection), trim ErrUnsupportedProjection docstring, regen new goldens. Use /tdd red then /gated-swarm Carmack+Linus. Spec is source of truth. Verify just test green + pending < 180 before close.
close_reason: Stage 3 landed on branch stage-3-projections. Model commit f053af8 (Projection sealed sum + AggregateFunc + Query.ReturnsAll, Linus gate PASS, pure refactor). Unlock commit (this): listener classifies var/var.prop/scalar-literal/func/aggregate + count(*) + RETURN *->ReturnsAll; 3 skiplist entries (NoVariablesInScope/UnknownFunction/ColumnNameConflict); +2 mustParse, -1 return-star mustReject, +1 authored residual; errors docstring trimmed; 10 new goldens; CONTEXT.md Projection/Aggregate + ADR 0003 note. TDD red->green, Linus gate PASS. godog 329/180 -> 336/173 (+7 passed, -7 pending). just test green. Follow-ups filed: gqlc-gyw (resolver grouping), gqlc-zir (unary-minus nit).

title: Stage 4 — multi-scope & UNION
description: Support WITH chaining (intermediate scopes that project the values Stage 3 introduces) and UNION branches. query.Query gains part/branch structure. Per ADR 0004 §Stages; explicitly depends on Stage 3.
close_reason: Stage 4 done: WITH chaining + UNION. Restructured query.Query into Branches(UNION)->Parts(WITH) with UnionKind enum; per-part scope threading (exported names L-to-R, WITH * transitive, cross-branch isolation); parameters stay query-wide. godog read-core 371 passed / 179 pending (was 336/173 at Stage 3 end; +35 scenarios parse, clauses/with+clauses/union added to readCoreDirs). 7 WITH/UNION negatives skiplisted (all verified below the type-interface boundary by adversarial review). Linus gate APPROVED. Commits 4491fb8(model)+b6cd944(unlock)+5821e4c(prep) pushed to origin/stage-4-with-union. NO PR per loop discipline.

title: Stage 2 — nullability (OPTIONAL MATCH)
description: Make bindings introduced by OPTIONAL MATCH nullable, and propagate that flag through the columns derived from them so codegen can emit pointer (or option) types post-freeze. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
close_reason: Stage 2 complete: nullable bindings, listener accepts OPTIONAL MATCH, Layer-2 + goldens updated. Spec doc at docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-2.md. Stack: stage-2-prep → stage-2-model → stage-2-unlock → stage-2-docs. just test green, godog 329/0/180.

title: Resolver: implicit grouping-key semantics for aggregate projections
description: Stage 3 (gqlc-npg) carries AggregateProjection with only its []Ref payload + the aggregate kind, deliberately NOT modelling implicit grouping (which non-aggregate RETURN columns form the group key). Per spec §9 (cypher-query-parser-stage-3.md) this is a resolver/Stage-4 concern entangled with WITH, and there is a real risk the []Ref-only payload proves insufficient and AggregateProjection must be reopened. This issue tracks adding grouping-key resolution once the model is frozen (gqlc-cta) and WITH lands (Stage 4, gqlc-v23).

title: Resolver: flow-type nullable bindings across clause structure
description:
Demote a binding's effective Nullable flag in the validated query when downstream clauses prove the binding must exist on every surviving row. Two regimes with different model dependencies:

(a) Edge-chain demotion (no new model structure beyond Stage 2 needed) — a non-nullable edge implies its endpoints exist, so chain 'non-nullable edge → non-nullable endpoints → demote those endpoints' nullability'. Handles the common case: OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b)-[:S]->(c) RETURN a, b, c — both a and b demote.

(b) Bare-pattern demotion (requires Stage 4 clause structure) — a required clause that reuses an OPTIONAL-introduced binding as a bare node/edge pattern filters NULL-rows. E.g. OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b) RETURN b. The model needs per-clause structure to record the second reference; Stage 4 (WITH/UNION) introduces that structure.

Per ADR 0006: the parser does not do this; the resolver does. (a) is shippable on its own after the freeze + resolver exists; (b) additionally waits on Stage 4.

title: docs: Stage 1 spec §6 — make 5→3 arithmetic explicit (Metz cycle-5 non-blocking note)
description: In docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-1.md §6 Layer-2 coverage gaps, the opening claim says ErrUnsupportedParameter is reachable by 5 fail-sites and the list shows 3 remaining uncovered. The intermediate isn't shown: 5 = 1 (cycle-introduced AUTHORED pin) + 1 (whole-map, corpus-covered by Match1[6] in mustReject) + 3 (uncovered). A reader counting 5-1=4 vs 3 bullets pauses unnecessarily. Insert one sentence between 'five distinct fail-sites' and 'the remaining three' that names Match1[6]'s coverage explicitly. Metz's exact suggested phrasing is in her cycle-5 diff review.

title: Stage 5 — undirected patterns
description: Support (a)-[r]-(b) patterns matching in either direction. Edge binding gains a direction-agnostic marker; resolution tries both orientations against the directed schema. May end up N/A if the schema stays directed-only — re-evaluate when reached. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
close_reason: Stage 5 complete: undirected relationship patterns (a)-[r]-(b) and (a)<-[r]->(b) now parse. EdgeBinding gains a positive 'directed' marker (field+Directed() accessor+trailing constructor param+always-emitted JSON key); listener uses an inverted zero-value-safe 'undirected' raw field, polarity flip at toBinding. collectEdge stops rejecting left==right; directed edges canonicalise, undirected keep textual order (no flip; <--> guarded). godog read-core 371/179 -> 380/170 (+9 passed). Linus gate APPROVED. Branch stage-5-undirected pushed to origin (commits dae7fb3 prep, b474fbe model, d4fbedd unlock). Not N/A: parser is schema-agnostic, marker is load-bearing (undirected yields no single EdgeKey, resolver forms two candidate keys).

title: Parser: unary-minus over a projection (RETURN -n.num) accepts as a plain ref
description: Linus review of Stage 3 unlock flagged that RETURN -n.num parses as a RefProjection to n.num rather than residual ErrUnsupportedProjection. Cause: the shared precedence-tower collapse (shape.go nonArithmeticFromAddSub) reads OC_UnaryAddOrSubtractExpression(0) without rejecting a leading '-'. Pre-existing behaviour governing Stage 0-2 too, not introduced by Stage 3, and no TCK read-core scenario exercises it — hence non-blocking. Decide whether a unary sign is arithmetic that should be residual (value lives below the type-interface boundary either way, ADR 0005) and tighten the helper if so.
